title: Idli
description: Soft, fluffy, steamed South Indian rice cakes prepared by fermenting rice and urad dal batter. A classic breakfast served with chutney and sambar.

ingredients.title: Ingredients (for ~25 idlis)
items: Parboiled rice (idli rice / ponni rice): 3 cups (600 g)
Whole white urad dal (black gram, skinless): 1 cup (200 g)
Poha (flattened rice): 2 tbsp (optional, for extra softness)

instruction.title: 1. Soaking
steps: Wash rice 3–4 times until water runs clear. Soak with enough water for 6 hours.
Wash urad dal and fenugreek seeds well. Soak together in water for 4 hours.
If using poha, soak separately in warm water for 20 minutes before grinding.

instruction.title: 2. Grinding (Restaurant-style precision)
steps: Urad dal: Grind in a wet grinder with minimal cold water, little by little, until it becomes light, fluffy, and 3x in volume (about 25–30 minutes).
Rice: Grind separately to a slightly coarse, grainy texture (like fine semolina). Add soaked poha during grinding for fluffiness.
Combine both batters. Mix thoroughly by hand (oxygen helps fermentation).

instruction.title: 3. Fermentation
steps: Transfer batter to a large steel vessel, leaving at least 40% space for rising.
Add salt only after fermentation (in warm climates) or before fermentation (in cold climates).
Keep covered in a warm, draft-free spot (ideal temperature: 28–32°C / 82–89°F) for 8–12 hours until batter doubles/triples and is airy.

instruction.title: 4. Steaming Idlis
steps: Grease idli moulds lightly with sesame oil.
Stir batter gently (do not overmix). Pour into moulds, ¾ full.
Steam in an idli steamer for 10–12 minutes on medium heat (lid on, without opening).
Check doneness: insert a wet spoon/knife—if clean, idlis are ready.
Rest for 2 minutes before unmoulding with a wet spoon.

chefTips: Urad dal ratio: High-quality restaurants use 1:3 urad-to-rice ratio for maximum fluffiness.
Poha trick: Adds softness (optional but recommended for restaurant-style).
Water quality: Always use filtered water; chlorine can hinder fermentation.
Fermentation booster (if climate is cold): Place the vessel inside an oven with the light on or near a warm spot.
Serving style: Serve with coconut chutney, tomato chutney, and hotel-style sambar. Garnish chutneys with tempered curry leaves, mustard seeds, and dry red chilies for an authentic restaurant feel.
